steps.title: Expand to standard quadratic form
steps.content: Expand the left side: $kx^2 - 112x = -16$. Move all terms to one side: $$kx^2 - 112x + 16 = 0$$ (Note: $k \neq 0$ for this to be quadratic.)

steps.title: Apply the discriminant condition for two distinct real solutions
steps.content: For a quadratic $ax^2 + bx + c = 0$ to have two distinct real solutions, the discriminant must be positive. With $a = k$, $b = -112$, $c = 16$: $$(-112)^2 - 4k(16) > 0$$ $$12544 - 64k > 0$$

steps.title: Solve for the maximum value of k
steps.content: From $12544 - 64k > 0$: $$12544 > 64k$$ $$k < \frac{12544}{64} = 196$$ Since $k$ must be an integer, the greatest possible value is $k = 195$.
